title: Stillingia bidentata
taxonomy: Stillingia bidentata is classified in the family Euphorbiaceae. This classification places it within the order Malpighiales. The family Euphorbiaceae is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the spurge family. Stillingia bidentata is native to the southeastern United States, where it is often found in wetland areas. The plant's classification reflects its relationship to other euphorbiaceous species, many of which are characterized by their milky sap.
uses: Stillingia bidentata is known for its potential medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
